free_text: Measurements of double differential neutron production
cross sections by 135 MeV/nucleon He, C, Ne and 95
- Target is natural. The enrichments of targets are
not given in the reference. But compiler asked
- Detector resolution: The energy resolutions of the
measured neutron spectra are about 3% for neutrons
- Efficiency: The neutron detection efficiencies were
calculated with the Monte Carlo code of Cecil et al.
for all energy ranges. References is R. A. Cecil, B.
D. Anderson, and R. Madey, Nucl. Instrum. Methods
Used as E counter, calibrated with a 60Co
The total normalization uncertainty is within 17%.
The statistical uncertainties vary from 2 to 5% for
the low- to medium-energy (about 10-100 MeV) region of
the spectra and increase to about 30% at the highest
evaporation model (HIC), See H.W.Bertini et al.,
ANG is polar angle between beam and neutron
